title: cydonia vulgaris f. maliformis
intro: Cydonia vulgaris f. maliformis, also known as the quince, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to Asia and southern Europe, characterized by its fragrant flowers and edible fruit. The medicinal properties of quince include its ability to soothe digestive issues and reduce inflammation, making it a popular remedy for stomach problems and menstrual cramps. The health benefits of quince include its potential to lower cholesterol levels and prevent the growth of certain types of cancer cells, as well as its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. Quince can be prepared into various herbal remedies, such as jams, jellies, and teas, which are often used to treat digestive issues, reduce fever, and promote relaxation.
